name: Molino de Gregorio Prieto and Chozo Fermín
name_original: Molino de Gregorio Prieto y refugio Chozo Fermín — circular desde Valdepeñas
description: Hike a moderate 11.9-mile (19.1 km) circular route from Valdepeñas, visiting the Molino de Gregorio Prieto and Refugio Chozo Fermín.
region_intro: Hiking around Valdepeñas offers trails through a landscape characterized by extensive vineyards, agricultural plains, and gentle riverine areas. The region, located in Castilla-La Mancha, features routes that often pass by historical landmarks and natural features such as the Arroyo del Peral. Terrain typically includes flat sections and mild ascents, making it accessible for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Valdepeñas The most popular hiking route is Molino de Gregorio Prieto and Chozo Fermín, a 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local countryside, passing by the Molino de Gregorio Prieto. Another top favourite among local hikers is The vineyards and the ancient baths of El Peral — circular, a difficult 15.2 miles (24.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the region's vineyards and leads to the ancient baths of El Peral. Local hikers also love the Gregorio Prieto Museum – Arroyo del Peral loop from Valdepeñas, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail leading through agricultural areas and along the Arroyo del Peral, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Valdepeñas is defined by its vineyards, agricultural plains, and river valleys, with a network of routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Valdepeñas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdepeñas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valdepeñas?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes around Valdepeñas, offering a variety of experiences. These include 19 easy trails, 19 moderate routes, and 1 more challenging option for experienced hikers.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on hiking trails around Valdepeñas?
A: The terrain around Valdepeñas is characterized by extensive vineyards, agricultural plains, and gentle riverine areas. You can expect mostly flat sections with mild ascents, making many routes accessible for various hiking abilities. Trails often follow rural paths and tracks through the countryside.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Valdepeñas?
A: Yes, Valdepeñas offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas – Plaza España loop from Valdepeñas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.4 km route that takes you through the town and its surroundings.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Valdepeñas?
A: For experienced hikers looking for a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199969050/the-vineyards-and-the-ancient-baths-of-el-peral-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The vineyards and the ancient baths of El Peral — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.5 km path. This route features more significant elevation changes and offers extensive views of the region's vineyards.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Valdepeñas?
A: Many hikes in Valdepeñas pass through vineyards and agricultural landscapes. You can also explore natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3451633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Peral&lt;/a&gt;, a riverine area known for its fertility. Historical landmarks such as the Molino de Gregorio Prieto are also points of interest along certain routes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valdepeñas?
A: Yes, Valdepeñas has several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gregorio Prieto Museum – Arroyo del Peral loop from Valdepeñas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.3 km trail that takes you through agricultural areas and along the Arroyo del Peral.

Q: Is Valdepeñas a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Given the prevalence of easy and moderate trails with generally mild ascents, Valdepeñas can be a good destination for family-friendly hikes. Routes often pass through open countryside, offering pleasant walks for all ages. Always check the specific trail details for distance and difficulty to ensure it suits your family's needs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valdepeñas?
A: The best time to hike in Valdepeñas is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during this season. Winters are usually mild, but check weather forecasts for rain.

Q: Are there options for parking at the trailheads in Valdepeñas?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, Valdepeñas is a town with general parking availability. For routes starting directly from the town, you can often find street parking or public car parks. For trails further afield, it's advisable to check local information or maps for designated parking areas near the starting points.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valdepeñas?
A: The hiking routes in Valdepeñas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet countryside, the scenic vineyard views, and the accessibility of the trails for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow river valleys or water features?
A: Yes, several trails in Valdepeñas incorporate riverine areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3451633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Peral&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gregorio Prieto Museum – Arroyo del Peral loop from Valdepeñas&lt;/a&gt; follow along this fertile area, offering pleasant walks by the water.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Valdepeñas?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many rural hiking trails in Spain, including those around Valdepeñas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural land or near livestock, and to clean up after them. Specific rules may apply in certain protected areas, so check local signage.
tips:
It is a reconstruction of a traditional mill.
At the entrance of the town, coming from Madrid, is the Molino Gregorio Prieto, a reproduction of a traditional windmill from La Mancha. The Mill is used as a museum and houses an interesting collection of objects from the popular tradition of Valdepeñas and offers typical gastronomic products from the area.

A very simple church. But really pretty. Actually, it was a nave in the enclosure of «Cachiporro» (Francisco de la Fuente Ruiz, a landowner from the beginning of the last century), which was used to perform the Eucharist since there was no church in that neighborhood at that time, being too far away the others; in fact, he was in charge of the chaplain of the Church of the Salesian nuns, practically in the center of the town and at a great distance.
From this point you can observe the views of the town of El Peral and where behind it is what is called the Sierra de los Bailones, in the background.

I'm not sure exactly what this structure is. It could be an old shepherd's shelter or some kind of oven from the past. It is very interesting because I have seen similar small shelters but never in this conical shape.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate hike, suitable for individuals with some hiking experience and a reasonable fitness level. It covers a distance of approximately 19.1 km with an elevation gain of about 94 meters.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable points of interest. You'll encounter the impressive Gregorio Prieto Windmill Museum, the religious building Santa María Magdalena, and the View of Sierra del Peral. Additionally, the trail passes by an Ancient Stone Shepherd's Hut.

faqs.question: What is special about the Molino de Gregorio Prieto?
faqs.answer: The Molino de Gregorio Prieto is renowned as the largest windmill in the world. Built in the 1940s, it was a gift to painter Gregorio Prieto to house his art collection focused on windmills. It now functions as a museum, showcasing his pictorial works and various models, and is a significant cultural landmark protected by the "Raigambre" Folkloric and Cultural Group. It was also the first museum in Valdepeñas.
